sound_type: jaw-pop -- loud tooth-clacking intimidation display. Broadband percussive 100-5,000 Hz. Defensive stress. Trail conflict. E=6
ecosystem: Forest, trail, campsite. Trail conflict zones. Defensive display context.
season: Year-round. Peak encounters spring through fall on trails.

coordinates.description: Black Bear throughout western Washington. Forest and trail zones. Common in Cascades foothills.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Black Bear entry. First at 200 (huff, E=5). Bear trio across 200-202. Jaw-pop is mechanical percussion, not vocalization.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Ursidae: Black Bear Huff (200, E=5), Black Bear Jaw-Pop (201, E=6), Black Bear Cub Distress (202, E=4).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Ursus from Latin 'bear'. americanus for American continent.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Ursus -- bears. Black Bear is the smallest North American bear.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Actually omnivorous -- 85% plant matter in PNW.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Ursidae: second entry. Bear trio at 200-202.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Largest PNW predator in lowland forests.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Broadband: 100-5,000 Hz. No single fundamental. Percussive transient.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Jaw-pop: broadband percussive. Huff: 50-300 Hz. Growl: 100-500 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Jaw-pop: 0.05-0.15 sec per pop. Often repeated at 1-3 per second.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single percussive transient per pop. Repeated in rapid bursts when agitated.
song_metrics.note_types: Jaw-pop (percussive, teeth), huff (exhalation, 200), growl (rare, extreme stress).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Escalation sequence: huff (200) -> jaw-pop (201) -> potential bluff charge. Multi-modal display.
song_metrics.timbre: Sharp, explosive, percussive. Like a heavy branch snapping. Teeth-on-teeth impact amplified by mouth cavity.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=6. Explosive intimidation. Louder than huff (E=5). Startling at close range. 50-100 m carry.
song_metrics.tempo: 1-3 pops per second when agitated. Single pops during mild stress. Rapid bursts during high agitation.
song_metrics.context: Defensive stress. Trail encounter. Food defense. Cub defense. Cornered bear. Always with other displays.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Jaw-pop mechanics are consistent across black bear populations.
song_metrics.key_property: JAW-POP. Percussion not vocalization. Teeth clacking. Broadband. E=6. BEAR TRIO 2/3 (200-202). ENERGY CONTRAST with Chastity Belt. v1.49.342.

pelage.overall: Black to dark brown (occasionally cinnamon or blonde in PNW). Muscular. Brown muzzle. No shoulder hump (distinguishes from grizzly).
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Black fur. Brown muzzle. No shoulder hump. Straight face profile (not dished like grizzly). During jaw-pop: visible saliva/foam, swaying head.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Thicker coat in fall/winter. Spring coat thinner after hibernation. Size varies with season (heaviest pre-hibernation).

physical_description.overall: Adult: 90-180 kg (200-400 lbs). 70-100 cm at shoulder. Black to brown fur. No shoulder hump. Omnivorous.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: No shoulder hump (vs. grizzly). Straight face profile. Black fur with brown muzzle. Smaller than grizzly.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-large bear. Black fur (usually). No shoulder hump. During defensive display: head swaying, ground slapping, jaw-popping, huffing. Do not approach. Back away slowly.

nodes.title: Jaw-Pop Mechanics -- Percussion Not Vocalization
nodes.description: Teeth clacking, mouth cavity amplification. A percussive instrument played by the bear's own skull. Broadband transient. Unique mechanism in the engine.

nodes.title: Escalation Ladder -- Huff to Jaw-Pop
nodes.description: E=5 to E=6. Awareness to threat. Behavioral escalation matches energy escalation. Multi-modal display: sound, movement, visual.

nodes.title: Bear Trio -- 200-202
nodes.description: Three entries, one species. Huff (awareness, E=5), jaw-pop (threat, E=6), cub distress (need, E=4). Escalation then inversion.

nodes.title: Trail Conflict -- Defensive Not Predatory
nodes.description: Bears that jaw-pop want distance. A bear that intends to attack is silent. The louder the display, the more stressed. Give space.

cross_references.relationship: BEAR TRIO 1/3! Huff (E=5) to jaw-pop (E=6). +1 escalation. Awareness to threat.
BEAR TRIO 3/3! Jaw-pop (E=6) to cub distress (E=4). Inversion. Adult intimidation to infant vulnerability.
BOTH E=6! Sea lion roar (sustained, resonant) vs. jaw-pop (percussive, explosive). Same energy, different mechanisms.
ENERGY CONTRAST! E=4 vs. E=6. Display (jaw-pop) and deflection (Chastity Belt). One demands. The other shrugs.
